Modern deep learning frameworks (like PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX) represent mathematical expressions as computational graphs. This abstraction enables the automatic and efficient computation of gradients required for backpropagation.

Graph Structure:

  • Nodes: Represent either variables (inputs, parameters) or operations (matrix multiplication, element-wise addition, non-linear activations).
  • Edges: Represent the dependencies between nodes, indicating the flow of multi-dimensional arrays (tensors) from one operation to the next.

sections.title: Illustration
sections.body: Nodes as operations, edges as tensor flows, caching forward values for reverse-mode autodiff.

sections.title: Forward and Backward Passes
sections.body:
During the forward pass, the graph is traversed topologically from inputs to outputs (the loss). Intermediate tensor values are computed and cached in memory.

During the backward pass (reverse-mode autodiff), the graph is traversed in reverse. At each node, the local Jacobian is evaluated uses the cached forward values, and is multiplied by the upstream gradient using the chain rule to continuously flow gradients back to the parameters.

If \( y = f(u) \) and \( u = g(x) \), the chain rule applied iteratively is:

\[ \frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = \frac{\partial y}{\partial u} \frac{\partial u}{\partial x} \]

sections.title: Static vs. Dynamic Graphs
sections.body:
• Static Graphs (Define-and-Run): The graph topology is defined entirely before execution (e.g., TensorFlow 1.x). This allows for aggressive ahead-of-time compiler optimizations (like operator fusion) but makes debugging and dynamic control flow (like variable-length loops) difficult.
  • Dynamic Graphs (Define-by-Run): The graph is constructed simultaneously as the forward pass executes on the fly (e.g., PyTorch). This allows arbitrary Python control flow (if statements, for loops) to dictate the graph's structure, offering vastly superior developer experience at a slight overhead cost.

title: Neural network
summary: A parameterized function approximator composed of layers of interconnected neurons, each applying an affine transformation followed by a non-linear activation, optimized iteratively over data using gradient descent.

papers.citation: 1943 · McCulloch, Pitts · A logical calculus of the ideas immanent in nervous activity

sections.body:
A parameterized function approximator composed of layers of interconnected neurons, each applying an affine transformation followed by a non-linear activation, optimized iteratively over data using gradient descent.

An artificial neural network maps an input vector \( x \in \mathbb{R}^{d_0} \) to an output \( \hat{y} \) by sequentially passing it through \( L \) hierarchical layers. At each hidden layer \( l \), the representation is transformed as:

\[ h^{(l)} = \sigma\!\left(W^{(l)} h^{(l-1)} + b^{(l)}\right), \quad h^{(0)} = x \]

where \( W^{(l)} \in \mathbb{R}^{d_l \times d_{l-1}} \) is the weight matrix mapping from dimension \( d_{l-1} \) to \( d_l \), \( b^{(l)} \in \mathbb{R}^{d_l} \) is the learnable bias, and \( \sigma \) is a required non-linear activation function (like ReLU or GELU). Without \( \sigma \), the entire composition of layers would algebraically collapse into a single linear map.

sections.title: Illustration
sections.body: A standard Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) mapping dense representations forward.

Universal Approximation Theorem: Cybenko (1989) and Hornik (1991) proved that a network with a single hidden layer of sufficient width, utilizing a non-polynomial activation function, can approximate any continuous function on a compact domain to arbitrary precision. However, this is strictly an existential proof: it does not guarantee that gradient descent can find the desired weights, nor bounding the required width mathematically, often requiring an infeasibly exponential number of neurons.

In practice, modern architectures rely entirely on depth rather than width; deeper architectures compose localized, hierarchal geometric warpings that are exponentially more parameter-efficient at representing complex natural manifolds (images, text) than wide, shallow networks.

Curse of Parameters: The sheer parameter density of fully connected networks scales quadratically with successive layer widths. For high-dimensional data like images, utilizing local structure via weight-sharing (CNNs) was paramount until compute capability enabled dense attention (Transformers) to implicitly learn structure.

title: Learning rate
summary: The scalar hyperparameter \( \eta \) (often \( \alpha \)) dictating the magnitude of parameter updates step size during gradient optimization, fundamentally controlling the trade-off between convergence speed and training stability.

papers.citation: 1986 · Rumelhart, Hinton, Williams · Learning representations by back-propagating errors

sections.body:
The scalar hyperparameter \( \eta \) (often \( \alpha \)) dictating the magnitude of parameter updates step size during gradient optimization, fundamentally controlling the trade-off between convergence speed and training stability.

In vanilla Gradient Descent, parameters \( \theta \) are iteratively updated by moving opposite the gradient of the loss \( \mathcal{L} \):

\[ \theta_{t+1} = \theta_t - \eta \nabla_\theta \mathcal{L}(\theta_t) \]

Choosing the correct learning rate is historically the most consequential hyperparameter decision in deep learning:

  • Too high: The optimizer will overshoot local minima. The loss will bounce erratically, or in worst-case scenarios, catastrophically diverge towards NaN.
  • Too low: The optimizer barely advances, making training prohibitively slow. It also increases the risk of the model settling in sharp, suboptimal local minima that fail to generalize well.
  • Sweet spot: It traverses steep areas efficiently, yet is small enough to settle into wide, flatter minima that generalize robustly to unseen data.

sections.title: Illustration
sections.body: 1D loss geometries demonstrating the consequence of varying step magnitudes.

sections.title: Learning Rate Schedules
sections.body:
Training rarely maintains a static learning rate. Instead, it alters over epochs via a schedule. Typical protocols include:

  • Warmup + Decay: Standard in Transformers. Linearly ramp the LR from 0 to \(\eta_0\) during the first 5-10% of training steps (preventing premature catastrophic gradients from uninitialized weights), followed by a gradual decay.
  • Cosine Annealing: Gradually decays \(\eta\) following a cosine curve \( \eta_t = \eta_{\min} + \frac{1}{2}(\eta_{\max} - \eta_{\min})(1 + \cos(\pi \frac{t}{T})) \), cleanly transitioning from exploration to exploitation.

sections.title: Adaptive Methods (Adam, RMSProp)
sections.body: Adaptive optimizers like Adam maintain a distinct effective learning rate for every single parameter by dividing the global \(\eta\) by the locally-estimated rolling variance \( \sqrt{\hat{v}_t} \) of gradients for that weight map. This dynamically tempers the step size for frequently changing parameters, dramatically reducing network dependence on fine-tuning the global \( \eta \).

title: Epoch
summary: One complete, comprehensive forward and backward pass of the entire training dataset through a machine learning algorithm, meaning every single example has contributed exactly once to a parameter update.

papers.citation: 1986 · Rumelhart, Hinton, Williams · Learning representations by back-propagating errors

sections.body:
One complete, comprehensive forward and backward pass of the entire training dataset through a machine learning algorithm, meaning every single example has contributed exactly once to a parameter update.

If a training dataset contains \( N \) examples and the optimizer partitions it into mini-batches of size \( B \), a single epoch will require \( \lceil N / B \rceil \) individual weight updates.

sections.title: Epochs, Steps, and Iterations
sections.body:
• Iteration / Step: A single update to the model weights computed from exactly one mini-batch.
  • Epoch: Enough iterations to exhaust the entire full dataset exactly once.
  • Total Compute Budget: Frequently expressed as (Epochs \( \times \) Dataset Size), or the total number of processed tokens (in language modeling).

sections.title: Dataset Shuffling
sections.body: At the boundary of every epoch, datasets are virtually always reshuffled. If the model was exposed to precisely the same mini-batches in the exact same sequence every epoch, Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) could develop cyclic update patterns that severely impede convergence. Shuffling guarantees random gradients that traverse the loss landscape more robustly.

sections.title: Diagnosing Performance via Epochs
sections.body:
Researchers monitor training health by mapping Training Loss and Validation Loss against the epoch integer:

  • Both descending cleanly: Model is successfully learning generalizable structure.
  • Train descending, Validation climbing: Severe overfitting. The model is memorizing the specific training examples rather than generalizing. Trigger early stopping or regularization.
  • Both completely plateaued: Learning has definitively stalled. This suggests either a learning rate issue, or that the model lacks the architectural capacity to model the complexity of the data.

sections.title: How many epochs?
sections.body: There is no mathematical constant. Small dense MLPs on MNIST might converge in 10 epochs. Massive Large Language Models (LLMs) often only run for 1 or 2 epochs over immense multi-trillion token corpora. The Chinchilla scaling laws argue that, historically, large networks were often trained for far too many epochs relative to their insufficient data volumes, discovering compute-optimal ratios.

title: Overfitting
summary: A modeling failure where the network learns the statistical noise and idiosyncratic details of its training data rather than the underlying generalizable distribution, yielding near-zero training error but catastrophic performance on uns…

papers.citation: 2019 · Belkin et al. · Reconciling modern machine learning practice and the bias-variance trade-off

sections.body:
A modeling failure where the network learns the statistical noise and idiosyncratic details of its training data rather than the underlying generalizable distribution, yielding near-zero training error but catastrophic performance on unseen data.

Bias–Variance Perspective:

Statistically, overfitting inhabits the high-variance, low-bias regime of the classical bias-variance decomposition. Because an overfit model is exceptionally complex, it contorts tightly to the training nodes (low structural bias), but its predictions swing wildly when presented with a slightly different dataset (high variance).

\[ \mathbb{E}\bigl[(y - \hat{f}(x))^2\bigr] = \text{Bias}(\hat{f})^2 + \text{Var}(\hat{f}) + \sigma^2 \]

sections.title: Illustration
sections.body: The quintessential diagnostic divergence distinguishing overfitting.

sections.title: Diagnostic Signature
sections.body: Overfitting is visually unmistakable on a training monitor. For the first stages, both training and validation losses descend continuously. Eventually, the validation curve stops improving, plateaus, and sharply diverges upwards as the model begins actively memorizing meaningless local noise, while the training loss continues coasting smoothly towards zero.

sections.title: Primary Causes
sections.body:
• The model possesses vastly more architectural capacity (parameters) than the structural complexity of the task requires.
  • A critically undersized training dataset.
  • Training models for an unnecessary number of epochs long after generalization has plateaued.

sections.title: Remedies
sections.body:
Technique Mechanism
L2 Weight Decay Adds \( \lambda \|\theta\|^2 \) to the loss function, aggressively penalizing excessively large parameter weights to keep the function smooth.
Dropout Randomly disables a percentage of neurons every forward pass. This prevents neurons from co-adapting and forces the network to learn robust redundancies.
Early Stopping Programmatically halts training the exact epoch the validation loss definitively begins increasing.
Data Augmentation Synthetically diversifies the dataset (flipping, cropping, blurring images), directly simulating a significantly larger unobserved dataset.

sections.title: The Double Descent Phenomenon
sections.body: Classical statistical theory posits that continuous increases in model size will inevitably produce fatal overfitting (the U-curve). Recent empirical evidence (Belkin et al., 2019) demonstrated that, surprisingly, if you scale capacity significantly larger than the interpolation threshold (where train loss hits zero), test error will shockingly descend a second time. This "Double Descent" justifies why hyper-parameterized, massive LLMs function seamlessly rather than explosively overfitting.

title: Underfitting
summary: A modeling failure where a network has insufficient architectural capacity or mathematical flexibility to capture the structural patterns in the training data, resulting in unacceptably high error on both training and test sets.

papers.citation: 1950s—present · Various

sections.body: A modeling failure where a network has insufficient architectural capacity or mathematical flexibility to capture the structural patterns in the training data, resulting in unacceptably high error on both training and test sets.

sections.title: Bias–Variance Perspective
sections.body:
Underfitting characterizes the high-bias regime. The model is so intrinsically simple or constrained that it introduces systematic structural biases that mathematically forbid it from approximating the actual unobserved data generating function, rendering more training data utterly useless.

An intuitive analogy is forcibly fitting a linear line \( \hat{y} = w_1 x + w_0 \) to a mathematically complex sinusoid pattern \( y = \sin(x) \). No choice of parameters can solve this representation constraint.

sections.title: Diagnostic Signature
sections.body: Unlike overfitting, where the training loss hits zero while validation skyrockets, an underfit model sustains terribly high loss on both splits. The learning curves will rapidly plateau at a high error magnitude early in training.

sections.title: Common Causes
sections.body:
• The network possesses far too few layers or hidden dimensions to map the hierarchical features of complex manifolds like natural language.
  • A missing/incorrect non-linear activation function, accidentally collapsing the entire network into a single linear map.
  • An excessively aggressive learning rate causing violent instability around the global minima.
  • Counter-productive levels of regularization (e.g. 80% Dropout rate, or massive \( L_2 \) Weight Decay).

sections.title: Direct Remedies
sections.body:
Strategy Resolution
Increase Model Capacity Increase hidden layer width, or deepen the layer count for inherently more complex topological representations.
Relax Regularization Temporarily eliminate Dropout and drastically lower \( \lambda \) weight decays to force the model to attempt memorization first.
Extend Compute Budget Simply increase the quantity of execution epochs; modern architectures sometimes require significant warm-up steps before descending the loss effectively.

title: Neuron
summary: The irreducible, foundational mathematical building block of a neural network: an independent node that computes a weighted sum of incident input signals, applies a scalar bias, and filters the result through a non-linear activation.

papers.citation: 1943 · McCulloch, Pitts · A logical calculus of the ideas immanent in nervous activity

sections.body:
The irreducible, foundational mathematical building block of a neural network: an independent node that computes a weighted sum of incident input signals, applies a scalar bias, and filters the result through a non-linear activation.

Given an arbitrary dimensional input vector \( x \in \mathbb{R}^n \), a solitary artificial neuron executes the formula:

\[ y = \sigma\!\left( w^\top x + b \right) = \sigma\!\left( \sum_{i=1}^{n} w_i x_i + b \right) \]

Where \( w \in \mathbb{R}^n \) serves as the learnable weight vector, \( b \in \mathbb{R} \) is the bias threshold moving the boundary away from the origin, and \( \sigma \) is any suitable non-linear activation mapping (e.g. ReLU, sigmoid).

The interior affine calculation \( z = w^\top x + b \) is universally referred to as the logit or pre-activation value.

sections.title: Illustration
sections.body: Affinely transforming inputs before applying a scalar non-linear filter \(\sigma\).

sections.title: Geometric Intuition
sections.body: If you remove the activation function, the continuous equation \( w^\top x + b = 0 \) rigorously defines a linear hyperplane bisecting the \( \mathbb{R}^n \) dimension. In Rosenblatt's Perceptron, testing which side of this dividing hyperplane an input fell upon served as a binary classifier. By wrapping the equation in a smooth activation, the neuron expresses a soft probabilistic gradient of confidence across the boundary line.

sections.title: Historical Origins
sections.body:
McCulloch and Pitts originated the formula in 1943 attempting to mathematically model biological synapses: dendrites ingest signals, the cellular soma sums them until surpassing an electrochemical threshold, whereupon the axon fires. Modern architectures have radically outgrown this analogy, leaning heavily into immense parallel geometries using dense matrices rather than modeling physical action potentials.

Python 
import numpy as np

def artificial_neuron(x, w, b, activation='relu'):
    # Inner Affine calculation (Pre-activation / Logit)
    z = np.dot(w, x) + b
    
    # Non-linear Filtering
    if activation == 'relu':
        return np.maximum(0, z)
    elif activation == 'sigmoid':
        return 1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(-z))

x = np.array([12.0, 5.0, 3.2])
w = np.array([0.5, -0.2, 0.9])
b = -0.5

print(f"Activation: {artificial_neuron(x, w, b, 'relu')}") # Output

title: Activation function
summary: A non-linear scale/filter mathematically applied to a neuron's affine pre-activation value, indispensable for allowing modern neural architectures to map highly complex, non-linear manifolds.

papers.citation: 2010 · Nair, Hinton · Rectified Linear Units Improve Restricted Boltzmann Machines

sections.body:
A non-linear scale/filter mathematically applied to a neuron's affine pre-activation value, indispensable for allowing modern neural architectures to map highly complex, non-linear manifolds.

Breaking the Linear Collapse:

Without independent non-linear activations, an arbitrary deep neural network identically unwinds back into a singular affine transformation matrix:

\[ W_L \cdots W_2 W_1 x = W_{\text{collapsed}} x \]

To acquire the Universal Approximation properties enabling deep-learning, the layers must geometrically buckle and fold the dimensional space. Activation functions uniquely warp the data sequentially, permitting multi-layer networks to construct intricate, tightly bound topological decision borders.

sections.title: Derivative Scale Determines Optimization
sections.body:
The derivative \( f'(z) \) of the selected activation definitively commands how efficiently backpropagation flows into deep layers via the chain rule.

  • Sigmoid's \( \sigma'(z) \): Caps strictly at \( 0.25 \). Multiplying tiny fractions \( 0.25 \) across layers exponentially pulverizes backward gradients into an untrainable Vanishing Gradient Minimum.
  • ReLU's \( f'(z) \): Is a perfect boolean \( 1 \) identically across all positive values, retaining robust, unscaled backpropagation directly through a network of essentially infinite depth.

sections.title: The Modern Compendium
sections.body:
Formula Output Domain Utility Protocol
ReLU: \( \max(0, z) \) \( [0, \infty) \) Sparsity generation. Prevents vanishing gradients entirely. Industry standard for Convolution / FC maps.
GELU: \( z \Phi(z) \) \( (-0.17, \infty) \) Smooth, non-monotonic approximation of ReLU blending probabilistic dropout. Default across all Transformer LLM architectures.
Sigmoid: \( \frac{1}{1+e^{-z}} \) \( (0, 1) \) Interpretable as absolute probability. Strict use-cases in Binary Classification layers, and information gating (e.g., LSTMs).

title: Sigmoid
summary: A classic differentiable S-shaped activation continuously mapping the entire real domain strictly bounded to \( (0, 1) \), universally interpretable as producing probabilistic confidence indices.

papers.citation: 1986 · Rumelhart, Hinton, Williams · Learning representations by back-propagating errors

sections.body:
A classic differentiable S-shaped activation continuously mapping the entire real domain strictly bounded to \( (0, 1) \), universally interpretable as producing probabilistic confidence indices.

The logistic sigmoid algebraically computes:

\[ \sigma(z) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-z}} \]

The output scales elegantly from \( \sigma(-\infty) = 0 \) to \( \sigma(+\infty) = 1 \). In neural applications, mapping negative numbers to 0 mathematically drops information identically to biological 'rest', while large activations represent boolean 'firing'.

sections.title: Derivative Collapse Mechanism
sections.body:
The derivative form expresses an elegant self-recursive factorization:

\[ \sigma'(z) = \sigma(z)\bigl(1 - \sigma(z)\bigr) \]

At its maximum mathematically possible velocity \( z = 0 \), \( \sigma'(0) = 0.25 \). Consequently, a backward pass through purely sigmoid layers enforces at minimum a 75% decay of signal per layer. Multiplying mathematically across \( L=10 \) layers obliterates gradients into the abyss (the Vanishing Gradient Problem), which rendered neural architecture stagnation a reality prior to ReLU architectures in 2012.

sections.title: Center Asymmetry (Zig-Zagging)
sections.body: Because outputs are exclusively positive, all downstream weights corresponding to an output layer will be mathematically forced to update via gradients bearing an identical sign (+ or - depending on the final loss delta). Optimization traverses in inefficient zigzag vectors to change multiple weights differently.

sections.title: Strict Modern Usage Profiles
sections.body:
Sigmoid is entirely excised from modern hidden layer stacks, surviving strictly as:

  • Binary Outcome: Serving the terminal logit in binary cross-entropy calculations representing true probability \( P(Y=1 | x) \).
  • Cellular Gating: Scaling arrays \( 0 \rightarrow 1 \) in LSTM Forget/Input gating to probabilistically mask/pass sequential information.
Derivations

Differentiating \( \sigma(z) = (1 + e^{-z})^{-1} \) using the chain rule yields:

\[ \sigma'(z) = -\bigl(1 + e^{-z}\bigr)^{-2} \cdot (-e^{-z}) = \frac{e^{-z}}{(1 + e^{-z})^2} \]

Factoring intuitively by injecting an algebraic \( +1 -1 \):

\[ \frac{1}{1+e^{-z}} \cdot \frac{e^{-z}}{1+e^{-z}} = \sigma(z) \cdot \frac{1+e^{-z}-1}{1+e^{-z}} = \sigma(z)\bigl(1 - \sigma(z)\bigr) \]

Python 
import numpy as np

def stable_sigmoid(z):
    '''Prevents exp overflow for extremely negative numbers'''
    z = np.clip(z, -500, 500)
    return np.where(z >= 0, 
                    1 / (1 + np.exp(-z)), 
                    np.exp(z) / (1 + np.exp(z)))

print(stable_sigmoid(np.array([0, 5, -500]))) 
# [0.5, 0.993, 0.0]

title: Tanh
summary: A strictly zero-centered sigmoidal activation function bounded geometrically to \( (-1, 1) \) resolving the asymmetrical gradient zig-zag defect of the logistic Sigmoid to accelerate training convergence.

papers.citation: 1998 · LeCun, Bottou, Orr, Müller · Efficient BackProp

sections.body:
A strictly zero-centered sigmoidal activation function bounded geometrically to \( (-1, 1) \) resolving the asymmetrical gradient zig-zag defect of the logistic Sigmoid to accelerate training convergence.

The hyperbolic tangent calculates:

\[ \tanh(z) = \frac{e^z - e^{-z}}{e^z + e^{-z}} \]

Mathematically, it identically replicates the geometry of the standard sigmoid but expansively re-scales and centers it over the zero origin:

\[ \tanh(z) = 2\sigma(2z) - 1 \]

sections.title: Derivative Dominance Over Sigmoid
sections.body:
Deriving the function yields:

\[ \tanh'(z) = 1 - \tanh^2(z) \]

The absolute derivative peaks at \( z=0 \) with \( \tanh'(0) = 1.0 \), four times stronger than Sigmoid's \( 0.25 \) peak gradient fraction. While it strictly remains susceptible to vanishing gradients once the values drift away toward massive saturation tails, the center of the function transmits early-layer gradient updates losslessly.

sections.title: The Zero-Center Optimization Advantage
sections.body: LeCun famously advocated the superiority of Tanh identically because it outputs values on both sides of zero. Downward gradients injected into identical upstream weight nodes will have mixed mathematical signs instead of strictly inheriting purely positive or negative scalar drift. The optimizer paths slice cleanly through parameter-space rather than zigzagging inefficiently over identical dimensions.

sections.title: Contemporary Utilities
sections.body:
• LSTM Internal State: The cell memory mapping utilizes bare Tanh: \( \tilde{c}_t = \tanh(W_c [h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_c) \), guaranteeing the internal cell sum \( (-1, 1) \) neither explodes identically nor collapses into zero permanently across sequential unrolls.
  • Data Stabilization: Serving as identical output boundaries in explicit generative pipelines preventing extreme artifacts (such as bounding generator outputs exactly across pixel representations).
  • Xavier Initialization Base: The classic Uniform Weight initialization standard derives directly assuming centered Tanh network expectations.
Derivations

Applying the standard quotient rule directly to \( t = \tanh(z) \):

Where \( u = e^z - e^{-z} \) and \( v = e^z + e^{-z} \):

\[ u' = v, \quad v' = u \]

\[ \frac{d}{dz}\tanh(z) = \frac{v \cdot v - u \cdot u}{v^2} = 1 - \left(\frac{u}{v}\right)^2 = 1 - \tanh^2(z) \]

Python 
import numpy as np

def tanh_derivative(z):
    t_val = np.tanh(z)
    return 1 - t_val**2

print(tanh_derivative(0)) # Returns 1.0
print(tanh_derivative(5)) # Returns ~0.00018 (Vanished)

title: ReLU
summary: The Rectified Linear Unit (ReLU) is a piecewise linear activation function defined as \( f(x) = \max(0, x) \). It became the default activation in modern deep networks because its constant derivative of 1 for positive inputs eliminates t…

papers.citation: 2010 · Nair, Hinton · Rectified Linear Units Improve Restricted Boltzmann Machines

sections.body:
The Rectified Linear Unit (ReLU) is a piecewise linear activation function defined as \( f(x) = \max(0, x) \). It became the default activation in modern deep networks because its constant derivative of 1 for positive inputs eliminates the vanishing gradient problem inherent to saturating activations like sigmoid.

Replacing smooth saturating activations with ReLU was one of the key historical breakthroughs (Nair & Hinton, 2010) that made training deep networks practical, as it allows backpropagating gradients to flow through infinite depth without exponential decay.

sections.title: Mathematical Definition & Gradient
sections.body:
Given an affine pre-activation \( x = W a + b \):

\[ f(x) = \begin{cases} x & \text{if } x > 0 \\ 0 & \text{if } x \le 0 \end{cases} \quad \implies \quad f'(x) = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if } x > 0 \\ 0 & \text{if } x < 0 \end{cases} \]

Note: The derivative is strictly undefined at \( x=0 \), but in software implementations (like PyTorch), it is conventionally hardcoded to 0. Since exactly zero is a measure-zero event for floating-point tensors, it does not impede stochastic descent.

sections.title: The Dying ReLU Problem
sections.body: If a large gradient update or heavily negative initialization pushes a neuron's affine output \( (Wa + b) \) consistently below zero for the entire dataset, the local derivative becomes 0. The neuron receives no gradient flow to ever recover, effectively dying and permanently removing capacity from the network.

sections.title: Modern Variations to Prevent Death
sections.body:
Variant Mathematical Definition Primary Advantage
Leaky ReLU \( \max(\alpha x, x) \) (static \( \alpha=0.01 \)) Allows a small, constant gradient when \( x < 0 \), ensuring dead neurons can eventually recover over training.
PReLU \( \max(\alpha_i x, x) \) (\( \alpha_i \) are learned parameters) Parametric. Lets the network learn the optimal negative slope per convolutional channel.
GELU \( x \cdot \Phi(x) \) Smooth, non-monotonic curve blending dropout probability. Crucial default in all Transformer architectures.

sections.title: Computational & Representational Sparsity
sections.body: Because ReLU clamps all negative values to absolute zero, roughly 50% of the neurons in a randomly initialized wide network output exactly 0. This extreme sparsity is highly computationally efficient and acts as a mild structural regularizer, forcing the network to represent concepts using distributed but sparse feature vectors.

title: Softmax
summary: The Softmax function converts a vector of arbitrary real numbers (logits) into a strict probability distribution by exponentiating each component and dividing by the sum of all exponents. It is the universally standard output activation…

papers.citation: 1959 · Luce (choice axiom) · Bridle (1990)

sections.body:
The Softmax function converts a vector of arbitrary real numbers (logits) into a strict probability distribution by exponentiating each component and dividing by the sum of all exponents. It is the universally standard output activation for multi-class classification and Attention architectures.

Given an unconstrained logit vector \( z \in \mathbb{R}^K \), Softmax normalizes it such that every output \( p_i \in (0, 1) \) and \( \sum p_i = 1 \):

\[ \text{softmax}(z)_i = \frac{e^{z_i}}{\sum_{j=1}^K e^{z_j}} \]

sections.title: Numerical Stability Shift
sections.body:
Because exponentiating large positive logits (e.g., \( e^{100} \)) causes floating-point overflow (`NaN`), numerical algorithms exploit Softmax's shift-invariance. Subtracting the absolute maximum element from all logits preserves the identical probabilities while ensuring the largest exponentiated term is elegantly \( e^0 = 1 \):

\[ \text{softmax}(z)_i = \frac{e^{z_i - \max(z)}}{\sum_j e^{z_j - \max(z)}} \]

sections.title: Gradient Synthesis with Cross-Entropy
sections.body:
Softmax is almost universally combined with Categorical Cross-Entropy (CCE) loss. Its partial derivative (a Jacobian matrix where off-diagonals are \( -p_i p_j \)) analytically perfectly cancels out the logarithmic terms in CCE. Thus, for a logit vector \( z \) and a one-hot ground-truth label \( y \):

\[ \nabla_z \mathcal{L} = p - y \]

This pristine, algebraically clean gradient \( p - y \) directly pushes the correct class logit upward proportionally to its current error, entirely bypassing the vanishing gradient pathologies associated with computing logarithms of sigmoid fractions independently.

sections.title: Temperature Scaling (\( \tau \))
sections.body:
Scaling the logits by a temperature \( \tau \) before Softmax governs the "sharpness" or entropy of the distribution:

\[ p_i = \frac{e^{z_i / \tau}}{\sum e^{z_j / \tau}} \]

  • \( \tau \to 0 \): Distribution collapses entirely into a deterministic Argmax (one-hot) vector.
  • \( \tau = 1 \): Standard raw Softmax probability.
  • \( \tau \to \infty \): Distribution smears into a completely flat Uniform distribution over all classes.

Temperature manipulation is paramount in LLM sampling constraints and Knowledge Distillation frameworks.

sections.title: Role in Transformer Attention
sections.body:
In Scaled Dot-Product Attention, Softmax collapses the raw \( QK^\top \) affinity scores into a normalized weighted routing matrix, allowing the Values \( V \) to be aggregated safely without exploding in magnitude:

\[ \text{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \text{softmax}\left(\frac{QK^\top}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right) V \]

title: Fully connected layer
summary: A Fully Connected (FC) layer (or Dense Layer) is the fundamental architectural building block where every input neuron structurally projects to every single output neuron via an independent learned parameter, executing an affine matrix t…

papers.citation: 1958 · Rosenblatt · The perceptron: a probabilistic model for information storage and organization in the brain

sections.body:
A Fully Connected (FC) layer (or Dense Layer) is the fundamental architectural building block where every input neuron structurally projects to every single output neuron via an independent learned parameter, executing an affine matrix transformation followed by a non-linearity.

Given an input vector or tensor squeezed into a sequence \( x \in \mathbb{R}^{d_{in}} \), the FC Layer computes:

\[ y = \sigma(Wx + b) \]

  • \( W \in \mathbb{R}^{d_{out} \times d_{in}} \): The dense weight matrix connecting every input to every output.
  • \( b \in \mathbb{R}^{d_{out}} \): The bias vector, effectively translating the hyperplane from the origin.
  • \( \sigma \): The element-wise activation function (e.g., ReLU), breaking the structural linear collapse constraint.

sections.title: Inductive Bias vs Generalization
sections.body: Unlike Convolutional layers (which bias towards spatial translation invariance) or Recurrent layers (which bias towards sequence temporality), Fully Connected layers possess zero structural priors. Because every dimension can arbitrarily interact with any other dimension without geometric restriction, they are theoretically capable of representing any complex conceptual entanglement.

sections.title: Parameter Overhead Pathology
sections.body: This absolute flexibility demands exorbitant parameter counts. The layer strictly requires \( (d_{in} \cdot d_{out}) + d_{out} \) parameters. If feeding a standard raw \( 1024 \times 1024 \) RGB image into a layer with \( 1000 \) neurons, the first \( W \) matrix alone demands over 3 billion parameters (\( 1024 \cdot 1024 \cdot 3 \cdot 1000 \)), instantly bankrupting modern VRAM before training even begins. This sheer density is precisely what motivated sparse convolutional abstractions in modern vision models.

sections.title: Modern Locality in Transformers
sections.body: In the seminal Attention mechanism, while the Attention module routes sequence data between tokens, the Feed-Forward Network (FFN) inside every block relies specifically on two massive Fully Connected layers operating on a strict per-token basis. This allows the FC layers to act as an immense, unconstrained associative memory bank, independently enriching each token's representation without cross-mixing positional data.

title: Input layer
summary: The Input Layer dictates the exact topological entry-point geometry for raw data entering a neural network. It possesses zero learnable parameters, functioning strictly to broadcast raw feature vectors forward into the first computationa…

papers.citation: Input layer

sections.body:
The Input Layer dictates the exact topological entry-point geometry for raw data entering a neural network. It possesses zero learnable parameters, functioning strictly to broadcast raw feature vectors forward into the first computational hidden layer.

If a network expects a raw feature vector \( x \in \mathbb{R}^{d_{in}} \), the input layer is mathematically defined as a pure identity passthrough:

\[ a^{(0)} = x \]

Because it executes no affine parameter operations or non-linear scaling, standard architectural notation excludes it from network depth (e.g., A "5-Layer CNN" contains 5 weight matrices, ignoring the input node structural definition).

sections.title: Data Geometric Definitions
sections.body:
Domain Model Input Geometry Standard Preprocessing
Tabular / MLP Flattened Vector: \( x \in \mathbb{R}^d \) Standardization (Z-score scaling to \( \mu = 0, \sigma^2 = 1 \)) to prevent high-magnitude features dominating initial gradients.
Vision / CNN 3D Tensor: \( (C, H, W) \) Pixel normalization from \( [0, 255] \) domain down to \( [0, 1] \) or \( [-1, 1] \).
NLP / Transformer Sequence of Integer IDs Passed identically to a dense Embedding layer lookup (where the true learnable transformation actually begins).

sections.title: Feature Scaling & Initialization Synergy
sections.body: The entire calculus of heuristic variance weight initialization (Xavier / He) rests on the strict assumption that the initial input layer \( x \) exhibits unit variance. If raw features wildly oscillate (e.g. house prices \( 500,000 \) alongside bedrooms \( 3 \)), the first affine projection matrix \( W_1 x \) instantly propagates massive activation instability, irreparably stalling convergence.

title: Hidden layer
summary: A Hidden Layer represents any intermediate representation mapping between the input and output layers. Depth strictly dictates how many latent affine and nonlinear transformations a dense feature manifold progresses through before the fi…

papers.citation: 1986 · Rumelhart, Hinton, Williams · Learning representations by back-propagating errors

sections.body:
A Hidden Layer represents any intermediate representation mapping between the input and output layers. Depth strictly dictates how many latent affine and nonlinear transformations a dense feature manifold progresses through before the final prediction classifier.

Each hidden layer \( l \) extracts progressively abstract topological correlations via parameterized multiplication and activation:

\[ h^{(l)} = \sigma(W^{(l)} h^{(l-1)} + b^{(l)}) \]

The "hidden" nomenclature historically emphasizes that intermediate vectors \( h^{(l)} \) are never strictly supervised (observed) by the static ground-truth labels during backpropagation training. They represent pure structural latent representations.

sections.title: Geometric Bottlenecks & Expansion
sections.body: By defining \( W \in \mathbb{R}^{d_{new} \times d_{prev}} \), the exact width \( d_{new} \) intrinsically regulates the channel capacity (information bottleneck) of that step in the graph. Expanding width temporarily scatters compressed features back into high-dimensional linear combinations for subsequent non-linear isolating, whereas constricting sequentially compresses identical feature variance into dense autoencoded manifolds.

sections.title: Continuous Composition (Abstract Hierarchies)
sections.body:
Zeiler and Fergus (2014) practically proved that sequential hidden transformations in an image classifier naturally learn a spatial visual hierarchy without any explicit human instruction.

  • Layer 1/2: Converges exclusively on Gabor filters (strict edge, color transition angles).
  • Layer 3/4: Geometrically combines lines into complex invariant loops/tires/noses.
  • Layer 5+: Fuses sub-components entirely to detect exact dog breeds or car chassis.

This automated hierarchical abstraction is categorically the central success of deep learning parameter graphs.

title: Output layer
summary: The final architectural layer of a neural network acting as the prediction head, where the deepest abstract hidden representation is linearly mapped onto an explicit Target Dimensionality, usually culminating in a specialized loss-pairin…

papers.citation: Output layer

sections.body:
The final architectural layer of a neural network acting as the prediction head, where the deepest abstract hidden representation is linearly mapped onto an explicit Target Dimensionality, usually culminating in a specialized loss-pairing operation like Softmax.

Operating explicitly on the final \( L \)-th layer abstracted representation \( h^{(L)} \), the pure output layer maps parameters to the strict dimensional demands of the designated metric:

\[ z = W_{out} h^{(L)} + b_{out} \]

sections.title: Standard Task & Parameter Pairings
sections.body:
The network's architectural formulation dictates the exact pairing of output non-linearity and backpropagation loss function necessary to correctly isolate probabilistic derivatives:

Objective Prediction Task Activation Function Paired Output Loss Matrix
Regression (e.g. Housing Price \( \$45,000 \)) Identity Line (No activation) Mean Squared Error (MSE)
Binary Classification Sigmoid: \( \frac{1}{1+e^{-z}} \) (Bounds exactly to \( 0-1 \)) Binary Cross-Entropy (BCE)
Multi-class Classification (or LLM Next-Token) Softmax: Normalizes all \( K \) vectors Categorical Cross-Entropy

sections.title: LLM Weight Tying (Vocabulary Bottlenecks)
sections.body:
In standard auto-regressive Transformers (GPT architectures), the final output layer is an enormous dense matrix projecting the local embedding dimension back across the massive Vocabulary length: \( W_{out} \in \mathbb{R}^{|V| \times d_{model}} \). Using a \( 50,000 \) token bank, this final projection matrix burns immense VRAM.

Weight Tying elegantly solves this by transposing the identical initial Input Embedding matrix \( E_{in} \) to serve exclusively as the Output fully connected matrix (\( W_{out} = E_{in}^\top \)), fundamentally slashing parameter cost while heavily regularizing the model by forcing tokens to structurally exist in identical geometric locations at entry and exit.

title: Multilayer perceptron (MLP)
summary: A Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) is the canonical feed-forward artificial neural network consisting of sequential fully-connected dense layers. Operating entirely on sequential non-linear abstraction, the MLP is both a historical foundation…

papers.citation: 1986 · Rumelhart, Hinton, Williams

sections.body:
A Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) is the canonical feed-forward artificial neural network consisting of sequential fully-connected dense layers. Operating entirely on sequential non-linear abstraction, the MLP is both a historical foundation and an indispensable expansion subroutine across modern architectures.

The standard deep-learning MLP strictly sequentially stacks parametric layers without temporal sequence loops (RNNs) or local spatial filters (CNNs):

\[ \hat{y} = f_L(f_{L-1}(\dots f_1(x))) \quad \text{where} \quad f_l(h) = \sigma(W^{(l)}h + b^{(l)}) \]

sections.title: The Universal Approximation Theorem
sections.body: Cybenko (1989) theoretically bounded that any continuous function across bounded domains can be mathematically approximated to arbitrarily dense precision using an MLP with just one massive hidden layer. However, the exact parameter width required is practically infinite, directly driving modern empirical proofs that extreme model depth represents identical continuous mapping combinations exponentially more efficiently than vast hidden layer width alone.

sections.title: Position-Wise Memory in Transformers
sections.body:
While strict isolated MLPs fail on high-dimensional images (collapsing via parameter parameter explosion), they remain the central structural component of sequential LLMs. Standard Transformer blocks alternate globally context-mixing Self-Attention with massive, strictly local parameter expansions called Position-Wise Feed-Forward Networks.

This FFN precisely follows modern MLP structures:

\[ \text{FFN}(x_{token}) = W_2 \, \sigma(W_1 x_{token} + b_1) + b_2 \]

Where \( W_1 \) explodes the model dimension (e.g., \( d \to 4d \)), and \( W_2 \) linearly projects the non-linearity explicitly back down. This dense expansion allows each token to privately compute isolated non-linear feature adjustments natively without distributing attention back into the sequence context vector.

title: Deep neural network
summary: An Artificial Neural Network possessing multiple hidden layers (typically \( L \ge 3 \)) mapping inputs to outputs. Depth is the defining structural characteristic of modern models, empirically granting them the capacity to learn highly…

papers.citation: 1986 · Rumelhart, Hinton, Williams

sections.body:
An Artificial Neural Network possessing multiple hidden layers (typically \( L \ge 3 \)) mapping inputs to outputs. Depth is the defining structural characteristic of modern models, empirically granting them the capacity to learn highly complex, hierarchical feature abstractions exponentially more efficiently than single-layer dense nets.

Mathematically, a deep network is explicitly a sequential continuous composition of non-linear functions:

\[ \hat{y} = f_L \circ f_{L-1} \circ \cdots \circ f_1(x) \]

sections.title: The Depth-Width Tradeoff
sections.body: While Hornik (1991) proved a single, infinitely wide hidden layer guarantees Universal Approximation over continuous domains, the parameter cost is catastrophic. Telgarsky (2016) quantified that a network of depth \( k \) naturally computes polynomials structurally demanding over \( \mathcal{O}(2^k) \) isolated nodes across a single shallow layer. Depth guarantees geometric abstraction efficiency.

sections.title: The Core Pathologies
sections.body:
Historically, deep architectures failed completely because of optimization degradation via the Chain Rule. Backpropagating a local gradient \( \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial W_1} \) back to early layers necessitates recursively multiplying Jacobians:

  • Vanishing Gradients: If the spectral norm of most weight matrices was \( < 1 \), or if bounded saturating activations (Sigmoids clamping derivatives explicitly at \( 0.25 \)) were used, the multiplied fraction decayed exponentially toward 0. Early layers froze permanently.
  • Exploding Gradients: If unregularized initialization pushed norms \( > 1 \), the gradients recursively scaled into `NaN` floating-point overflow.

sections.title: Enabled Modern Deep Scaling
sections.body:
Modern ResNets effortlessly achieve 152+ layers, while standard LLaMA deployments effortlessly stack upwards of 80 full sequential Transformer blocks. This was technically unlocked by:

Innovation Gradient Preservation Origin
ReLU Perfect \( 1.0 \) non-decaying positive gradient. 2010 (Nair & Hinton)
Careful Initialization He/Xavier mathematically forces the variance output by dense matrices explicitly towards 1 to prevent exploding. 2010 (Xavier), 2015 (He)
Residual Pass-throughs \( f(x) + x \): Adding skip connections guarantees an explicit identity path (\( 1.0 \)) completely bypassing the vanishing blocks entirely to the deep root. 2015 (He et al.)

title: Deep learning
summary: The dominant paradigm within Machine Learning built explicitly around exploiting deep cascaded neural networks natively processing raw data points. Its core tenet universally replaces hard-coded human feature engineering with end-to-end…

papers.citation: 2012 · Krizhevsky, Sutskever, Hinton · ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks

sections.body:
The dominant paradigm within Machine Learning built explicitly around exploiting deep cascaded neural networks natively processing raw data points. Its core tenet universally replaces hard-coded human feature engineering with end-to-end learnable internal representation abstractions.

Classical ML models cleanly separated feature pipelines: Ph.D. researchers spent decades mathematically engineering SIFT matrices, Gabor edges, or TF-IDF NLP bounds, eventually feeding these handcrafted, dense features into simple regressors (SVMs or Random Forests).

Deep Learning entirely subsumes this architecture. The network operates directly on primitive inputs (raw localized pixel grids or distinct Byte-Pair word segments). Sequential layers empirically construct independent progressive features:

  • \( f_1(x) \): Extracts dense Gabor edges/line gradients automatically.
  • \( f_2(x) \): Combines invariant lines into structural geometric loops.
  • \( f_5(x) \): Detects entire abstract Dog noses or human vocal chords intrinsically.

sections.title: The Triad of Supremacy
sections.body:
Although backpropagation was formalized in 1986, shallow SVM algorithms vastly outperformed networks for 25 years. Deep Learning became dominant around 2012 (AlexNet) immediately driven by a singular triad:

Triad Core Modern Instantiation Historical Limitation Solved
High-dimensional Datasets The compilation of ImageNet (\(14\text{M}+\) supervised images) and uncontrolled internet-crawled text data sets (Pile). Overparameterized networks flawlessly memorized small sets. Dense data physically forces them to learn deep invariant hierarchies to compress loss perfectly.
Hardware Computation Nvidia GPUs providing massive matrix-multiplication SIMD acceleration clusters. Collapsed unfeasible months of CPU gradient updates directly down into immediate hour cycles.
Architectural Algorithmic Shifts Non-saturating Activations (ReLU), Normalization Bounds, Transformer Attention Heads. Formally defeated the Vanishing Gradient problem destroying dense \( L>3 \) topologies.

title: Composite function
summary: A composite function is formally defined by sequentially assigning the mathematical output of one distinct operation explicitly as the required geometric input to the next \( (g \circ f)(x) = g(f(x)) \). Entire Neural Networks mathematic…

papers.citation: Composite function

sections.body:
A composite function is formally defined by sequentially assigning the mathematical output of one distinct operation explicitly as the required geometric input to the next \( (g \circ f)(x) = g(f(x)) \). Entire Neural Networks mathematically compress into a singular, massively deep continuous composite function.

Given successive dense affine transformations combined with explicit non-linear breaking operations \( \sigma \), the abstract definition of a network is strictly composed depth:

\[ \hat{y} = F(x) = (\sigma_3 \circ W_3 \circ \sigma_2 \circ W_2 \circ \sigma_1 \circ W_1)(x) \]

sections.title: Backpropagation and The Chain Rule
sections.body:
The entire calculus feasibility of Deep Learning exists solely because of the mathematical derivative properties isolated in continuous composite functions. The Chain Rule precisely isolates local Jacobian gradients for immediate computation.

The global gradient isolated to update layer 1 is simply the exact sequential multiplication of strictly local sub-gradients cascaded backward from the isolated loss layer:

\[ \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial W_1} = \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial \hat{y}} \cdot \frac{\partial \hat{y}}{\partial h_2} \cdot \frac{\partial h_2}{\partial h_1} \cdot \frac{\partial h_1}{\partial W_1} \]

This isolates intense, global matrix calculations down into computationally cheap \( O(1) \) modular operations natively executed identically on Tensor Cores.

sections.title: Non-Linear Geometries vs Linear Overlap
sections.body: Critically, linear functions fail composite isolation. Composing two independent matrices \( f(x) = W_1 x \) and \( g(y) = W_2 y \) identically collapses structurally into a single useless \( \hat{y} = (W_2 W_1) x \). This is precisely why introducing distinct \( ReLU \) or \( GELU \) scaling explicitly prevents linear overlapping. Activating functions force the compositional topology to buckle heavily, explicitly permitting multi-layer networks to map enormously complicated dimensional border separations the shallow Perceptron could never trace natively.

title: Feedforward neural network
summary: An artificial neural network where data flows strictly in a uni-directional forward pass from input to output. The computational graph is a strict Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) entirely devoid of recurrent cycles or temporal feedback loops.

papers.citation: Feedforward neural network

sections.body: An artificial neural network where data flows strictly in a uni-directional forward pass from input to output. The computational graph is a strict Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) entirely devoid of recurrent cycles or temporal feedback loops.

sections.title: Illustration

sections.body:
The standard Feedforward Neural Network (FFN) encapsulates traditional MLPs and CNNs. Given an input \( x \in \mathbb{R}^{d_0} \), each succeeding layer strictly computes its state from the immediately preceding depth:

\[ h^{(l)} = \sigma\left(W^{(l)} h^{(l-1)} + b^{(l)}\right) \]

sections.title: Computational Determinism
sections.body: Because there are no cycles, the forward pass cost is completely deterministic and strictly bounded by the total number of operations: \( \mathcal{O}(\sum_l ||W^{(l)}||_0) \). Backpropagation traverses this exact graph exactly once in reverse. This contrasts sharply with Recurrent architectures, which must dynamically unroll across sequence time-steps, accumulating theoretically unbounded memory footprint and depth.

sections.title: Expressive Capacity & Universal Approximation
sections.body: Historically, Cybenko (1989) proved that an FFN with a singular non-linear hidden layer is a Universal Approximator, theoretically capable of matching any continuous function over a compact set. However, the width \( d_1 \) required grows exponentially. Modern FFNs rely on extreme depth (dozens to hundreds of sequential layers) to hierarchically construct feature spaces parametrically cheaper.

sections.title: The FFN inside the Transformer
sections.body:
While global sequences are managed by Attention, every single token in a Transformer is subsequently rushed through an isolated dense FFN subroutine:

\[ \text{FFN}(x) = W_2 (\text{GELU}(W_1 x)) \]

This strict feedforward bottleneck is the primary mathematical instrument enabling multi-layer Transformers to execute non-linear memory retrieval independent of positional sequence mixing.

title: Convolutional neural network (CNN)
summary: A highly specialized feedforward architecture heavily biased toward unstructured spatial/grid data (like images) that utilizes isolated sliding discrete convolutional filters instead of global dense matrix multiplication, drastically red…

papers.citation: 1998 · Yann LeCun · Gradient-Based Learning Applied to Document Recognition

sections.body: A highly specialized feedforward architecture heavily biased toward unstructured spatial/grid data (like images) that utilizes isolated sliding discrete convolutional filters instead of global dense matrix multiplication, drastically reducing parameters via structural weight-sharing.

sections.title: Illustration

sections.body:
A Convolutional Neural Network assumes strict spatial locality. Rather than a dense \( W \) connecting every input pixel, a CNN sweeps a small 2D kernel matrix (e.g. \( 3 \times 3 \)) across the entire input grid, generating feature maps via discrete 2D cross-correlation:

\[ S(i, j) = (I * K)(i, j) = \sum_m \sum_n I(i-m, j-n) K(m, n) \]

sections.title: The Dual Inductive Biases
sections.body:
This architecture is defined by two foundational priors mathematically mirroring biological visual cortex mechanics:

  1. Local Connectivity: Instead of global semantic patterns, the kernel exclusively isolates localized pixel gradients (edges, textures).
  2. Translation Equivariance (Weight Sharing): Exactly the same kernel \( K \) is applied identically at every \( (i, j) \) coordinate. If a "cat ear" filter activates in the top-left, sliding the image translates the identical activation to the bottom-right. \( f(Shift(x)) \approx Shift(f(x)) \).

sections.title: Parameter Efficiency Collapse
sections.body: A dense layer processing a small \( 256 \times 256 \) RGB image to output merely \( 1000 \) hidden nodes requires an impossible \( \sim 196,000,000 \) parameters for the first layer alone. A CNN layer utilizing 64 discrete \( 3 \times 3 \) filters computes immense localized feature maps using exactly \( \sim 1,728 \) parameters (\( 3 \cdot 3 \cdot 3 \cdot 64 \)), regardless of the input resolution's vastness.

sections.title: Historical Ascension (LeCun & AlexNet)
sections.body: Originating with LeCun's digit-recognizing LeNet-5 (1998), CNNs exploded to empirical dominance when Krizhevsky's AlexNet (2012) mapped these localized convolutions massively in parallel across GPU hardware alongside the ReLU activation, instantly annihilating decades of manual computer-vision feature-engineering and officially launching modern Deep Learning.

title: Recurrent neural network (RNN)
summary: A neural network topology featuring explicit cyclical feedback loops, enabling the system to maintain an internal temporal "Hidden State" memory across unbounded sequential data steps.

papers.citation: 1986 · Rumelhart, Hinton, Williams · 1990 · Elman

sections.body: A neural network topology featuring explicit cyclical feedback loops, enabling the system to maintain an internal temporal "Hidden State" memory across unbounded sequential data steps.

sections.title: Illustration

sections.body: Unlike Feedforward networks, Recurrent Neural Networks natively process sequence variables \( x_t \) by injecting the output of step \( t-1 \) identically back into the calculation of step \( t \).

sections.title: Mathematical Formulation
sections.body:
The standard "Vanilla" RNN simultaneously computes a temporal hidden state \( h_t \) and an output prediction \( y_t \):

\[ h_t = \sigma(W_{hx} x_t + W_{hh} h_{t-1} + b_h) \]

\[ y_t = W_{yh} h_t + b_y \]

sections.title: Temporal Weight Sharing
sections.body: Crucially, the exact parameter matrices \( W_{hx} \) and \( W_{hh} \) are strictly identical and shared absolutely across every single time step \( t \). This allows the RNN to process text or time-series variables of theoretically infinite length without expanding parameter size, providing an intense inductive bias toward temporal shift invariance.

sections.title: Optimization Catastrophe (BPTT)
sections.body:
Training requires unrolling the sequence loop horizontally into a massively deep feedforward graph and calculating Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT). The gradient traversing backward from \( t=100 \) to \( t=1 \) forces the exact recursive multiplication of local Jacobians \( 100 \) times:

\[ \frac{\partial h_t}{\partial h_{t-k}} = \prod_{i=t-k+1}^{t} W_{hh}^\top \text{diag}(\sigma'(z_i)) \]

This causes fatal exponential variance. If the structural spectral radius of \( W_{hh} \) deviates from \( 1.0 \), the gradient either radically explodes into `NaN` or crashes to \( 0 \) (Vanishing Gradients), preventing the standard RNN from learning dependencies further than 5-10 temporal steps apart. This mathematical failure necessitated the invention of LSTMs.

title: Elman RNN
summary: The foundational "Simple RNN" conceptualized by Jeffrey Elman (1990) where a dedicated "context layer" continuously holds a copied copy of the network's continuous hidden activation to act strictly as temporal input memory on the success…

papers.citation: 1990 · Jeffrey Elman · Cognitive Science · Finding Structure in Time

sections.body:
The foundational "Simple RNN" conceptualized by Jeffrey Elman (1990) where a dedicated "context layer" continuously holds a copied copy of the network's continuous hidden activation to act strictly as temporal input memory on the successive time step.

The Elman architecture defined the structural baseline for sequence learning. While the preceding Jordan network (1986) fed the network's final output probabilities \( y_{t-1} \) back inside, Elman brilliantly fed back the abstract, high-dimensional intermediate layer: the Hidden State.

sections.title: Mathematical Formalism
sections.body:
The Elman RNN explicitly concatenates the current input vector with the immediately preceding latent abstraction vector:

\[ h_t = \tanh(W_{hx} x_t + W_{hh} h_{t-1} + b_h) \]

sections.title: Representational Impact vs Jordan Network
sections.body:
• Jordan (Output Feedback): Feeding back \( y_{t-1} \) forces the mathematical state to strictly represent external task goals (e.g., probability of the noun "car" limits the memory payload to precisely \( K \) classes).
  • Elman (Hidden Feedback): Feeding back \( h_{t-1} \) allows the model unconstrained dimensional space to develop a highly rich, uninterpretable latent encoding holding vast historical nuance rather than just immediate prediction data.

sections.title: Historical Significance
sections.body: Elman originally proved that when trained predicting succeeding characters in standard English sentences, these hidden layers naturally arranged themselves into incredibly clean hierarchical clustered branches without explicitly programmed rules (distinguishing strictly between inanimate objects, verbs, pluralities, etc.). It definitively proved backpropagation could synthesize inherent complex grammatical logic from raw sequential input observation alone, laying the groundwork for all modern NLP.

title: Hidden state
summary: The Hidden State is the dense latent vector maintained by a neural network's intermediary layers encapsulating its currently computed uninterpretable belief about the input data. Specifically in Recurrent models, it acts as the primary t…

papers.citation: 1986 · Rumelhart, Hinton, Williams · Learning representations by back-propagating errors

sections.body: The Hidden State is the dense latent vector maintained by a neural network's intermediary layers encapsulating its currently computed uninterpretable belief about the input data. Specifically in Recurrent models, it acts as the primary temporal memory bottleneck carrying the entire encoded sequence history forward.

sections.title: Feedforward Hierarchy (CNNs / MLPs)
sections.body: In standard Feedforward topologies, the individual layer output vectors \( h^{(l)} \in \mathbb{R}^{d_l} \) continuously serve as the transient hidden state. These latent tensors are rigorously unobservable in the final output prediction space \( \hat{y} \), and mathematically translate raw pixels/inputs into high-dimensional semantic clustering planes (e.g. recognizing edges \( \to \) eyes \( \to \) faces).

sections.title: Recurrent Sequence Bottlenecks (RNNs)
sections.body:
In Recurrent Neural Networks, the vector \( h_t \in \mathbb{R}^d \) structurally functions as the sole temporal transmission unit:

\[ h_t = \tanh(W_{hx} x_t + W_{hh} h_{t-1} + b_h) \]

Crucially, because \( h_t \) contains a strictly fixed dimensionality \( d \), it acts as a massive theoretical Information Bottleneck. After continuously absorbing \( 1,000 \) words in a document paragraph, it is forced to compress every single syntactic, semantic, and structural relationship down into an identical tiny array of \( 256 \) floats. This catastrophic compression failure directly drove the invention of the Transformer.

sections.title: Transformer Residual Streams
sections.body:
Transformers (BERT, GPT) explicitly abolish the temporal transmission hidden state entirely. Instead, they maintain a Residual Stream hidden state natively at every isolated token position \( i \) simultaneously: \( h_{i}^{(l)} \in \mathbb{R}^{d_{model}} \). The tokens attend backward dynamically without compressing past context into a recursive singularity bottleneck.

Note: Alain & Bengio (2017) demonstrated that "linear probing" distinct hidden states across deep NLP models perfectly exposes at which exact intermediate layer the model internally comprehends grammar versus object-permanence.

title: Backpropagation through time (BPTT)
summary: Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) is the algorithmic prerequisite for training Recurrent networks. It physically "unrolls" the cyclic temporal graph horizontally into an immensely deep linear feedforward network spanning \( T \) time s…

papers.citation: 1990 · Paul Werbos · Backpropagation Through Time: What It Does and How to Do It

sections.body:
Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) is the algorithmic prerequisite for training Recurrent networks. It physically "unrolls" the cyclic temporal graph horizontally into an immensely deep linear feedforward network spanning \( T \) time steps, permitting the standard chain-rule derivative calculation to traverse backward across history.

When computing exactly how the shared recurrent hidden matrix \( W_{hh} \) contributed to an error occurring at step \( t \), the gradient must traverse backward through the entire history to step \( k \):

\[ \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}_t}{\partial W_{hh}} = \sum_{k=1}^{t} \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}_t}{\partial h_t} \cdot \left( \prod_{j=k+1}^{t} \frac{\partial h_j}{\partial h_{j-1}} \right) \cdot \frac{\partial h_k}{\partial W_{hh}} \]

sections.title: The Pathological Core (Multiplicative Jacobians)
sections.body:
The exact center product term \( \left( \prod_{j=k+1}^{t} \frac{\partial h_j}{\partial h_{j-1}} \right) \) is mathematically defined as repeatedly multiplying the local step Jacobians: \( W_{hh}^\top \text{diag}(\sigma'(z_j)) \).

If processing a modest sentence of 50 words, this demands multiplying the exact identical hidden weight matrix \( W_{hh} \) by itself \( 50 \) consecutive times. Equivalently approaching \( (W_{hh})^{50} \). Because standard activation derivatives (tanh/sigmoid) are strictly bounded fractional multipliers (e.g. \( < 1.0 \)), and spectral norms rarely precisely balance, this continuous multiplication invariably forces the entire gradient formula to unconditionally crash toward absolute 0 (Vanishing Gradients) or multiply into Infinity `NaN` (Exploding Gradients) long before it provides any adjustment to token \( k=1 \).

sections.title: Truncated BPTT (TBPTT)
sections.body: Practically addressing the memory footprint and explosion pathologies, engineering frameworks (PyTorch) almost never unroll a full textbook sequence. TBPTT explicitly severs the computational gradient graph horizontally after a fixed boundary \( \tau \) (e.g. unrolling every 25 steps, detaching the history tensors, and updating). It wildly reduces RAM constraints at the severe theoretical expense of totally blinding the model to causal updates longer than \( \tau \).

title: What is the vanishing gradient problem?
summary: An optimization pathology absolutely devastating deep neural architectures where the backward-propagating loss gradients progressively decay exponentially toward zero through successive multiplicative Chain Rule layers, permanently paral…

papers.citation: 1991 · Sepp Hochreiter · Untersuchungen zu dynamischen neuronalen Netzen

sections.body:
An optimization pathology absolutely devastating deep neural architectures where the backward-propagating loss gradients progressively decay exponentially toward zero through successive multiplicative Chain Rule layers, permanently paralyzing the network's earliest structural layers from receiving weight updates.

During fundamental Feedforward or sequence BPTT backpropagation, computing the explicit parameter gradient for an early initial layer \( l \) strictly forces compounding the local derivative Jacobians for every single layer deeper in the model:

\[ \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial W^{(1)}} = \left( \prod_{k=2}^{L} \frac{\partial h^{(k)}}{\partial h^{(k-1)}} \right) \cdot \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial \hat{y}} \cdot \frac{\partial h^{(1)}}{\partial W^{(1)}} \]

sections.title: The Mathematical Cause (Saturating Non-Linearities)
sections.body:
Historically, Networks universally deployed the smooth Sigmoid \( \sigma(z) \) function due to biological intuition. However, its strict derivative equation tops out at a theoretical absolute maximum of \( \sigma'(z) \dots \le 0.25 \).

Tracing backward through an immensely modest 6-layer CNN:

\[ 0.25 \times 0.25 \times 0.25 \times 0.25 \times 0.25 = 0.000976 \]

The gradient physically reaching Layer 1 to adjust the foundational edge-detection filters is pulverized to less than \( \frac{1}{1000} \text{th} \) of the final output error magnitude. Layer 1's weights simply freeze natively, stranding the model completely at initialization and destroying convergence likelihood.

sections.title: Architectural Salvations
sections.body:
The dominant innovations in the final decade exclusively revolved around bypassing this recursive multiplicative fraction collapse:

Technique Gradient Preservation Strategy
ReLU Activation Mathematically imposes a perfect constant \( f'(x) = 1.0 \) specifically for all positive signals, eliminating the fractional 0.25 multiplier penalty completely.
He / Xavier Initialization Calculates starting random matrices precisely so the structural variance exiting every layer strictly identically mirrors its input variance.
Residual Skip Connections Bypasses multiplicative blocks natively by structuring an Additive graph \( h^{(l)} = f(x) \textbf{ + x} \). The derivative of identical \( \textbf{+ x} \) is explicitly \( 1.0 \), carving a direct unadulterated gradient superhighway from layer 150 safely backward to Layer 1.
LSTM Gates (RNN) Operates an entirely additive cell state \( C_t \) using "Forget gate" parameters empirically preserving temporal Jacobians precisely near \( \approx 1.0 \).

title: Long short-term memory (LSTM)
summary: A radically modified recurrent architecture engineered explicitly to bypass the Vanishing Gradient problem by partitioning the traditional hidden layer into a purely Additive Cell State regulated entirely by learned, multiplicative sigmo…

papers.citation: 1997 · Sepp Hochreiter, Jürgen Schmidhuber · Long Short-Term Memory

sections.body:
A radically modified recurrent architecture engineered explicitly to bypass the Vanishing Gradient problem by partitioning the traditional hidden layer into a purely Additive Cell State regulated entirely by learned, multiplicative sigmoid gates.

Rather than compounding non-linearities directly mathematically \( h_t = \sigma(W x_t + W h_{t-1}) \), the LSTM (Hochreiter & Schmidhuber, 1997) isolates a protected foundational memory ribbon — the Cell State \( C_t \) — spanning the entire chronological sequence alongside the standard Hidden State \( h_t \).

sections.title: The Gating Equations
sections.body:
The network structurally defines three independent \( [0, 1] \) Sigmoid gates interacting identically against the concatenated sequence input \( [h_{t-1}, x_t] \):

  • Forget Gate \( f_t = \sigma(W_f[h, x] + b_f) \): Dictates exactly what fractional magnitude of the previous cell state \( C_{t-1} \) must be physically erased.
  • Input Gate \( i_t = \sigma(W_i[h, x] + b_i) \): Dictates exactly what fractional magnitude of the newly proposed candidate cell \( \tilde{C}_t \) should mathematically be incorporated into the permanent ribbon.
  • Output Gate \( o_t = \sigma(W_o[h, x] + b_o) \): Filters the permanent internal Cell state \( C_t \), deciding identically what portion of the memory is actively useful to emit right now as the visible Hidden State \( h_t \).

sections.title: Additive Cell State Update
sections.body:
The actual memory update isolates the gates algebraically via element-wise matrix multiplication (Hadamard Product \( \odot \)):

\[ C_t = f_t \odot C_{t-1} + i_t \odot \tilde{C}_t \]

\[ h_t = o_t \odot \tanh(C_t) \]

sections.title: Solving the BPTT Gradient Collapse
sections.body: Crucially, because the central Cell State \( C_t \) advances through time using strict addition (\( + i_t \odot \tilde{C}_t \)) rather than dense matrix multiplication, traversing back across 500 sequence steps via the Chain Rule isolates the local Jacobian purely to the Forget Gate term \( f_t \). If the Forget Gate activates at \( 1.0 \), the BPTT derivation \( \frac{\partial C_t}{\partial C_{t-1}} \) identically equals \( 1.0 \). Gradients flow completely unhindered backward across infinite theoretical sequences without vanishing.

title: xLSTM
summary: An advanced "Extended" modification of the classical 1997 LSTM introduced directly by its original creator (Hochreiter, 2024), utilizing exponential gating functions and massive matrix-memory topologies mathematically engineered specific…

papers.citation: 2024 · Sepp Hochreiter et al. · xLSTM: Extended Long Short-Term Memory

sections.body:
An advanced "Extended" modification of the classical 1997 LSTM introduced directly by its original creator (Hochreiter, 2024), utilizing exponential gating functions and massive matrix-memory topologies mathematically engineered specifically to compete comprehensively against state-of-the-art Transformer Large Language Models.

While standard Transformers completely replaced recurrent architectures universally in 2017 by eliminating sequential bottlenecks during training, they intrinsically require debilitating \( \mathcal{O}(N^2) \) quadratic temporal inference computations. The xLSTM revives the \( \mathcal{O}(N) \) Linear-Time inference recurrent structure by drastically modernizing two core variants: sLSTM and mLSTM.

sections.title: 1. Exponential Gating (sLSTM - Scalar LSTM)
sections.body:
Standard LSTMs utilized biological Sigmoid gates bounding values identically between \( [0, 1] \), inherently placing a hard structural cap on the mathematical magnitude a state could be weighted (a strict upper bound of 1.0). The sLSTM structurally ditches Sigmoid in favor of Exponential Gating.

\[ i_t = \exp(W_i[x,h]) \]

This formally grants the network effectively infinite dynamic range. Because exponentials scale boundlessly, an sLSTM cell can dramatically and explicitly over-weight specific extremely salient context tokens (like a rare keyword) exponentially higher than irrelevant background sequences, directly mirroring the sharp attention matrices calculated heavily inside a Transformer.

sections.title: 2. Matrix Memory Cells (mLSTM)
sections.body:
A conventional LSTM structurally stores memory purely inside an isolated \( 1D \) Vector \( C_t \). The mLSTM replaces this limited vector fundamentally with a massive \( 2D \) Covariance Matrix Memory, utilizing an outer-product update logic exactly mimicking the Key/Value mathematical mappings in Attention:

\[ C_t = f_t \cdot C_{t-1} + i_t \cdot (v_t \otimes k_t) \]

The output is physically retrieved mechanically by multiplying a Query vector directly against this stored covariance matrix \( C_t q_t \). Thus, it directly acts as a massively compressed linear attention mechanism operating inside a continuous \( \mathcal{O}(1) \) hidden state updating organically over time.

title: minLSTM
summary: A radically stripped-down "Minimal" LSTM variant mathematically severing all hidden-state dependencies from its gating functions, completely destroying the standard Recurrent auto-regressive sequential bottleneck and immediately enabling…

papers.citation: 2024 · Leo Feng, Yoshua Bengio et al. · Were RNNs All We Needed?

sections.body:
A radically stripped-down "Minimal" LSTM variant mathematically severing all hidden-state dependencies from its gating functions, completely destroying the standard Recurrent auto-regressive sequential bottleneck and immediately enabling massive temporal parallelization via prefix-scans during hardware training.

The most catastrophic modern failure of traditional LSTMs strictly versus Transformers rests upon Training Parallelizability. Within a standard LSTM, generating the Hidden State \( h_{t=100} \) inherently requires waiting computationally for the explicit generation of \( h_{t=99} \) to define the Forget Gate \( f_{100} \). Training is forced sequentially sequentially, crippling massive GPU core utilization.

sections.title: Severing the Backwards Dependence
sections.body:
The minLSTM entirely removes the \( h_{t-1} \) vector entirely from the gating equations. The Forget and Input gates depend strictly exclusively upon the isolated individual input token \( x_t \) available instantaneously at step 0:

\[ f_t = \sigma(W_f x_t + b_f) \]

\[ i_t = \sigma(W_i x_t + b_i) \]

Additionally, the minLSTM entirely drops the Output element gate, structurally collapsing the standard additive update sequence directly into an explicit convex ratio combining the historical state identically against the newly proposed Hidden input:

\[ h_t = \frac{f_t}{f_t + i_t} \odot h_{t-1} + \frac{i_t}{f_t + i_t} \odot (W_h x_t + b_h) \]

sections.title: Parallel Prefix Scans
sections.body: Because the gating variables \( f_t \) and \( i_t \) can now be calculated simultaneously for every single token \( [x_1 \dots x_N] \) globally across the entire trillion-token dataset entirely concurrently, the final update function translates perfectly into an associative global Prefix-Sum. This collapses the crippling \( O(T) \) sequential temporal traversal directly back into the structurally parallel \( O(T \log T) \) computational acceleration class utilized exclusively by modern linear state-space models like Mamba and S4.

title: Gated recurrent unit (GRU)
summary: A gated recurrent unit (GRU) is a recurrent cell that adaptively interpolates between copying old memory and writing new content using only two gates. It keeps the long-term gradient path of gated RNNs but removes LSTM's separate cell st…

papers.citation: 2014 · Kyunghyun Cho, Bart van Merrienboer, Dzmitry Bahdanau, Yoshua Bengio · Learning Phrase Representations using RNN Encoder-Decoder for Statistical Machine Translation 2014 · Junyoung Chung, Caglar Gulcehre, Kyunghyun Cho, Yoshua Bengio · Empirical Evaluation of Gated Recurrent Neural Networks on Sequence Modeling 2015 · Rafal Jozefowicz, Wojciech Zaremba, Ilya Sutskever · An Empirical Exploration of Recurrent Network Architectures

sections.body:
A gated recurrent unit (GRU) is a recurrent cell that adaptively interpolates between copying old memory and writing new content using only two gates. It keeps the long-term gradient path of gated RNNs but removes LSTM's separate cell state, so it often reaches similar accuracy with fewer parameters and faster training.

Given input \(x_t \in \mathbb{R}^d\) and previous hidden state \(h_{t-1} \in \mathbb{R}^n\), the GRU computes:

\[ z_t = \sigma(W_z [h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_z), \quad r_t = \sigma(W_r [h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_r) \]

\[ \tilde{h}_t = \tanh\!\left(W_h [r_t \odot h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_h\right), \quad h_t = (1-z_t) \odot h_{t-1} + z_t \odot \tilde{h}_t \]

where \(\sigma\) is sigmoid, \(\odot\) is element-wise multiplication, and \([\cdot,\cdot]\) is concatenation. The update gate \(z_t\) sets the memory timescale: small \(z_t\) preserves old state (long memory), large \(z_t\) overwrites with fresh evidence. The reset gate \(r_t\) controls how strongly the past participates in candidate construction; if \(r_t \approx 0\), the candidate ignores most prior state and behaves like a near-input-driven feature extractor.

Intuitively, GRU can be viewed as a learned exponential smoothing process per hidden dimension. In dimensions where sequence statistics are stable, training tends to keep \(z_t\) small, creating nearly identity recurrence and better gradient flow through time; in rapidly changing dimensions, \(z_t\) opens and allows quick state updates. This adaptive timescale mechanism is why GRUs are robust on medium-length dependencies despite their simpler parameterization.

Aspect GRU LSTM
State variables Single \(h_t\) \(h_t\) and \(c_t\)
Gate count 2 (update, reset) 3 (input, forget, output)
Affine transforms per step 3 4
Typical trade-off Lower parameter/FLOP budget, often similar quality More expressive gating, sometimes better on very long dependencies

In practice, GRUs are common when latency or memory matters (embedded ASR, compact sequence models, time-series forecasting). For large-scale NLP, self-attention largely displaced recurrent cells, but GRU remains a strong baseline when sequence lengths are moderate and compute is constrained.

Derivations

Write the recurrence as \(h_t = h_{t-1} + z_t \odot (\tilde{h}_t - h_{t-1})\). This makes explicit that each coordinate performs a gated residual update toward the candidate state. If \(z_t^{(i)} \approx 0\), coordinate \(i\) behaves close to identity and contributes a near-unity temporal Jacobian term, helping preserve gradients.
